name: Mock Fish Buddhist
recipeIngredient: 1 large potato; cooked, peeled and sliced ¼ inch thick
10 wood ears; soaked to soften, tough ends removed and cut into slivers
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the potato slices with flour.
2. Deep-fry until golden brown.
3. Drain and set aside.
4. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of oil, then reheat.
5. Add onion and stir-fry for 10 seconds.
6. Add snow peas and wood ears; stir-fry another 10 seconds.
7. Add salt, sugar, and water. Bring to a rapid boil, stirring constantly, until peas are just tender crisp.
8. Add reserved fried potato slices, heat through, and serve.
Wood ears are a type of mushroom or shelf fungus. When soaked, it has a crunchy, gelatinous texture with little taste. If you can't find them, dried mushrooms could be used as a substitute, though they wouldn't provide the exact effect. A closer substitute would be dried jellyfish, but if you're in an area that sells dried jellyfish, they likely have wood ears as well.
